title: salix grandifolia macrophylla
intro: Salix grandifolia macrophylla, also known as the bigleaf willow, is a large, deciduous shrub or small tree with broad, lance-shaped leaves that are typically 4-8 inches long. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its high salicin content, a compound similar to aspirin. The health benefits of Salix grandifolia macrophylla include relief from pain and inflammation, as well as the potential to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and salves, which can be made by steeping the bark or leaves in hot water or by mixing them with a carrier oil.
